name: TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BACKLOG-3
title: Dispatcher should always pass --no-human-approval to subagent since it owns the gate
description:
In non-autonomous mode, the dispatcher does the HUMAN-APPROVAL gate (SKILL.md Step 4) and then dispatches the subagent WITHOUT --no-human-approval. This leaves Step 2 in the subagent prompt as dead code — the subagent silently can't pause and skips through, but the user already approved at the dispatcher so it is functionally harmless.

Fix: have the dispatcher always pass --no-human-approval to dispatch-tdd-subagent.py (since the dispatcher always owns the gate). The flag's meaning shifts from 'user wants autonomous mode' to 'dispatcher gate already fired OR user wants autonomous mode'. Remove or stub out the Step 2 HUMAN-APPROVAL section from the subagent prompt template in dispatch-tdd-subagent.py for the non-autonomous case.

Related: TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BUG-2 (closed — architectural fix already in place; this is the dead-code cleanup).

history.note: Incorrect premise. --no-human-approval must only be passed when the user has explicitly granted bypass permission in-conversation. Always passing it would silently eliminate the human approval gate, defeating its purpose. Current design is correct: dispatcher passes --no-human-approval only on explicit user authorization.
